items.title: Verbazing bij Heracles: 'Niemand gehoord die dit geen rood vindt'
items.summary: Heracles Almelo incasseerde zaterdag een mokerslag in de Eredivisie . De ploeg van trainer Ernest Faber verloor in eigen huis van hekkensluiter NAC Breda en kwam zo zelf weer op de laatste plaats terecht. Na afloop ging het over het niveau van de wedstrijd en de arbitrage.

items.title: VI Live: Ajax tikt Fortuna scheel, Godts maakt 3-0
items.summary: Ajax neemt het zaterdagavond om 20.00 uur op tegen Fortuna Sittard. De Amsterdammers hopen de draad weer op te pakken na het puntenverlies tegen Excelsior en AZ. Volg de wedstrijd op de voet in de VI Live.

items.title: Basisdebuut wordt drama voor Zinchenko: Ajax-aanwinst raakt snel geblesseerd
items.summary: Oleksandr Zinchenko is al in beginfase van de thuiswedstrijd van Ajax tegen Fortuna Sittard geblesseerd uitgevallen. De 29-jarige Oekraïner was zaterdagavond voor het eerst basisspeler bij de Amsterdammers.

items.title: VI Live: Meerdink maakt rentree bij AZ, opmerkelijke rode kaart Bizot
items.summary: Zaterdag staan er vier Eredivisie-duels op het programma. Voor Ajax tegen Fortuna Sittard is er een apart liveblog, voor de rest kun je terecht in deze VI Live. En uiteraard ook voor het belangrijkste voetbal in het buitenland.

items.title: Aston Villa v Newcastle: FA Cup fourth round – live
items.summary: Updates from 5.45pm (GMT) game at Villa Park Follow us over on Bluesky | And get in touch: email Barry 1 min: Tammy Abraham gets the ball rolling, playing it a few yards backwards to Amadou Onana. Within seconds it finds its way to the feet of Villa goalkeeper Marco Bizot. Not long now: Kieran Trippier and Lucas Digne skipper the sides, which are led out on to the Villa Park pitch by referee Chris Kavanagh and his team of match officials soundtracked by Ozzy Osbourne’s Crazy Train. Kick-off is just a couple of minutes away. Continue reading...

items.title: Liverpool v Brighton: FA Cup fourth round – live
items.summary: Updates from 8pm kickoff (GMT) at Anfield Follow us over on Bluesky | And get in touch: email Scott Liverpool make four changes to their starting line-up after their 1-0 win at Sunderland. Dominik Szoboszlai returns from suspension, while Curtis Jones, Milos Kerkez and Federico Chiesa all step up. Hugo Ekitike, Ryan Gravenberch and Andrew Robertson drop to the bench, while Wataru Endo is injured. Brighton make three changes to their starting XI following their 1-0 defeat at Aston Villa. Jason Steele replaces Bart Verbruggen in goal, while Charalampos Kostoulas and 17-year-old Harry Howell come in for Kaoru Mitoma and Danny Welbeck, surprisingly dropped to the bench. Continue reading...

items.title: Forever Young superb once again in Saudi Cup
items.summary: Forever Young created history when becoming the first dual winner of the world’s most valuable horse race, the Saudi Cup.

items.title: Offside? Abraham scores controversial opener for Villa
items.summary: A well-worked free-kick routine is finished off by Tammy Abraham for his first goal since returning to Aston Villa, but the replays show the goal would have been ruled out for offside had VAR been in use.

items.title: Grim dolblij met Zinchenko: 'Een pure aanwinst voor Ajax'
items.summary: Fred Grim is dolgelukkig met de komst van Oleksandr Zinchenko bij Ajax. Volgens de trainer van de Amsterdammers heeft de Oekraïner nu al een leidersrol op zich genomen.

items.title: From 'demoralising' 8-0 defeat to pushing Man City all the way
items.summary: Thirteen months after being embarrassed 8-0 by Manchester City, Salford City produce a performance to make their fans proud.

items.title: Kane nets 500th goal, Bayern cruise past Bremen
items.summary: Harry Kane scored twice to help Bayern Munich to a 3-0 win against Werder Bremen in the Bundesliga on Saturday.

items.title: Man City latest: Pep unimpressed by ‘boring’ FA Cup win over Salford
Kane reaches 500 goals - what other records could he break?
items.summary: Bayern Munich striker Harry Kane became the first Englishman to reach 500 career goals on Saturday, but what other records could he break?

items.title: Reborn Scotland regain Calcutta Cup in England's latest Murrayfield nightmare
items.summary: Scotland unleashed another Murrayfield nightmare on England as they regained the Calcutta Cup - and eased the pressure on Gregor Townsend - with a 31-20 victory that extinguished their rivals' 12‑Test winning run.

items.title: Mansfield cause FA Cup upset against Burnley
items.summary: Burnley were on the wrong end of an FA Cup upset as they were beaten 2-1 by Mansfield on Saturday.

items.title: Ronaldo ends protest, scores 18 mins later
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo returned to Al Nassr's lineup for Saturday's match at Al Fateh -- and swiftly scored.

items.title: Man City see off Salford 2-0 in FA Cup 4th round
items.summary: Manchester City reached the fifth round of the FA Cup with a far-from-convincing 2-0 win over League Two side Salford on Saturday.

items.title: Tottenham appoint Igor Tudor as new head coach
items.summary: Tottenham Hotspur have appointed Igor Tudor as their new head coach, the club have confirmed.

items.title: Southampton and Norwich win all-Championship ties to progress in FA Cup
Mansfield's Reed scores fabulous free-kick to upset Burnley
items.summary: Watch Mansfield Town's Louis Reed produce "set-piece sorcery," scoring a sensational free kick to stun Premier League side Burnley and reach the FA Cup fifth round.
